zh.lifeSummary: 當Margaret de Ludlow於大約1325年出生在英國林可爾史爾史可萊維利斯比時，她的父親Thomas de Ludlow I61歲，母親Joane II De Marmion42歲。她於大約1354年與John Dymoke在英國林可爾史爾史可萊維利斯比結婚。他們至少是7個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世於1415年3月16日在她的家鄉享年91歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭林可爾史爾史可萊維利斯比。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Margaret de Ludlow于大约1325年出生在英国林可爾史爾史可萊維利斯比时，她的父亲Thomas de Ludlow I61岁，母亲Joane II De Marmion42岁。她于大约1354年与John Dymoke在英国林可爾史爾史可萊維利斯比结婚。他们至少是7个儿子和2个女儿的父母。她去世于1415年3月16日在她的家乡享年91岁，埋葬在英国英格兰林可爾史爾史可萊維利斯比。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Ludlow
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Irish: habitational name from a place in Shropshire, so named from the Old English river name Hlūde (from hlūd ‘loud, roaring’) referring to the Teme river + hlāw ‘hill’. This name was taken Ireland in the 13th and 17th centuries. See also Laidlaw .

variants.name: Laidlaw
meanings: Scottish: Border surname found mainly around Selkirk and the vales of Ettrick and Yarrow; a Scottish development of   Ludlow  .

variants.name: Ledlow
meanings: English, Irish, or Scottish: variant of   Ludlow   or   Laidlaw  .

variants.name: Ludlam
meanings: English: possibly from a misreading of w as m in Ludelawe, a recorded medieval spelling of the placename Ludlow in Shropshire. Compare Ludlow .

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Margaret de Ludlow was born about 1325, in Scrivelsby, Lincolnshire, England, her father, Thomas de Ludlow I, was 61 and her mother, Joane II De Marmion, was 42. She married John Dymoke about 1354, in Scrivelsby, Lincolnshire, England. They were the parents of at least 7 sons and 2 daughters. She died on 16 March 1415, in her hometown, at the age of 91, and was buried in Scrivelsby, Lincolnshire, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Margaret de Ludlow est née environ 1325, à Scrivelsby, Lincolnshire, Angleterre, son père, Thomas de Ludlow I, avait 61 ans et sa mère, Joane II De Marmion, avait 42 ans. Elle a épousé John Dymoke environ 1354, à Scrivelsby, Lincolnshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 7 fils et 2 filles. Elle est décédée le 16 mars 1415, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 91 ans, et a été enterrée à Scrivelsby, Lincolnshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als Margaret de Ludlow um 1325 in Scrivelsby, Lincolnshire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Thomas de Ludlow I 61 und ihre Mutter Joane II De Marmion 42. Sie hat um 1354 in Scrivelsby, Lincolnshire, England John Dymoke geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 7 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Sie starb am 16. März 1415 im Alter von 91 Jahren in ihrem Geburtsort und wurde in Scrivelsby, Lincolnshire, England, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Margaret de Ludlow è nata il circa 1325 nel Scrivelsby, Lincolnshire, England, sua padre, Thomas de Ludlow I, aveva 61 anni e sua madre, Joane II De Marmion, aveva 42 anni. Lei sposato John Dymoke circa 1354, nel Scrivelsby, Lincolnshire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 7 figli e 2 figlie. Morì il 16 marzo 1415, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 91 anni, e fu sepolta a Scrivelsby, Lincolnshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Margaret de Ludlowが約1325年にイギリスリンコルンシュアイアスクリベルスビーで生まれた時，父Thomas de Ludlow Iは61歳，母Joane II De Marmionは42歳であった。約1354年にイギリスリンコルンシュアイアスクリベルスビーでJohn Dymokeと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも7人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼女は1415年3月16日に故郷で91歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスリンコルンシュアイアスクリベルスビーにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Margaret de Ludlow(이)가 대략 1325년 영국 링코실르 스크라이벨스비에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Thomas de Ludlow I(은)는 61세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Joane II De Marmion(은)는 42세였다. 그녀는 대략 1354년 영국 링코실르 스크라이벨스비에서 John Dymoke(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 일곱 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1415년 3월 16일 그녀의 고향에서 91세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 링코실르 스크라이벨스비에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Margaret de Ludlow nasceu aproximadamente 1325, em Scrivelsby, Lincolnshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Thomas de Ludlow I, tinha 61 anos e sua mãe, Joane II De Marmion, tinha 42 anos. Ela casou-se com John Dymoke aproximadamente 1354, em Scrivelsby, Lincolnshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 7 filhos e 2 filhas. Ela faleceu em 16 de março de 1415, em sua cidade natal, com 91 anos, e foi sepultada em Scrivelsby, Lincolnshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Margaret de Ludlow родилась около 1325 г. в местности скривелсби, линколншир, Англия, её отцу по имени Thomas de Ludlow I было 61 год, а матери, которую звали Joane II De Marmion, 42. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени John Dymoke около 1354 г. в местности скривелсби, линколншир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 7 сыновей и 2 дочери. Она умерла 16 марта 1415 г. в её родном городе в возрасте 91 год и была похоронена в местности скривелсби, линколншир, Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Margaret de Ludlow nació aproximadamente 1325, en Scrivelsby, Lincolnshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Thomas de Ludlow I, tenía 61 años y su mamá, Joane II De Marmion, tenía 42 años. Se casó con John Dymoke aproximadamente 1354, en Scrivelsby, Lincolnshire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 7 hijos y 2 hijas. Murió el 16 de marzo de 1415, en su pueblo, a la edad de 91 años, y fue sepultada en Scrivelsby, Lincolnshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
